sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Futures

items.headline: U.S. Futures Decline Amid Rising Middle East Tensions and Iran Deadline
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are broadly lower in pre-market trading this morning, as investors react to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is a key focus, contributing to market caution. Crude oil prices are notably higher, reflecting supply concerns. The market sentiment is characterized by wariness, with participants closely monitoring developments.

items.headline: Tesla Shares See Pre-Market Decline
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) is showing a notable decline in pre-market trading, reflecting broader market cautiousness.

items.headline: NVIDIA Edges Up in Early Trading
items.summary: NVIDIA ($NVDA) is trading slightly higher in pre-market, suggesting some resilience in the technology sector despite overall market weakness.

items.headline: Limited Individual Stock Pre-Market Data
items.summary: Comprehensive real-time pre-market data for individual top gainers and losers is limited at this early hour (8:11 AM UTC). The provided movers reflect available indications. Investors should note that pre-market movements can be highly volatile and may not predict regular trading session performance.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Dominate Global Market Focus
items.summary: Geopolitical risks in the Middle East remain the primary driver of market sentiment. President Trump's Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is creating significant uncertainty, with crude oil prices surging as a result of potential supply disruptions.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Climb Ahead of Iran Deadline
items.summary: Brent crude is trading above $111 a barrel, and WTI crude is over $114 a barrel, with both showing significant gains. This surge is directly linked to the escalating tensions in the Middle East and concerns over oil supply.

items.headline: Key Economic Data and Treasury Auctions Scheduled for Today
items.summary: Today's economic calendar includes several Treasury auctions: an 8-Week Bill Offering Amount at 6:00 AM ET, 17-Week and 4-Week Bill Offering Amounts at 10:00 AM ET, and a 3-Year Note Total Accepted Bids at 12:00 PM ET. Additionally, investors anticipate Durable Goods Orders at 8:30 AM ET and Consumer Credit at 3:00 PM ET. European services sector PMI data for March is also expected.

items.headline: Several Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Xcel Brands ($XELB) and Direct Digital ($DRCT) are scheduled to release their Q4 2025 earnings and host conference calls later today. LG Electronics Inc. also announced record preliminary Q1 2026 earnings. Levi Strauss ($LEVI) is also expected to report earnings.

items.headline: Energy Sector in Focus, Tech Shows Resilience in Asia
items.summary: With crude oil prices climbing, the energy sector is under close watch. Globally, technology stocks in Asia have shown some resilience, leading gains in those markets, suggesting potential for similar trends elsewhere as they are viewed as less exposed to geopolitical conflicts.
